violations.claim: The function is supposed to return false when the pattern is not present.
violations.smt2:
; LINE: 109
; PRINCIPLE: P5  ; Semantic Correctness – the comment states the exact pattern that must be detected.
; The function is supposed to return false when the pattern is not present.
; We again use Boolean‑like integer flags for the two asserts.

(declare-const eqPresent   Int)   ; 1 ⇔ "(assert (= … …))" occurs
(declare-const nePresent   Int)   ; 1 ⇔ "(assert (not (= … …)))" occurs
(declare-const result      Int)   ; return value: 1 = true, 0 = false

; Flags are 0/1
(assert (or (= eqPresent 0) (= eqPresent 1)))
(assert (or (= nePresent 0) (= nePresent 1)))
(assert (or (= result 0) (= result 1)))

; ----- Desired property from the comment -----
;   result = 1  ⇔  (eqPresent = 1 ∧ nePresent = 1)
; Its negation (bug) is: result = 1  ∧  (eqPresent = 0 ∨ nePresent = 0)

(assert (= result 1))
(assert (or (= eqPresent 0) (= nePresent 0)))

; No additional constraints – the implementation could set `result` to true
; without verifying the two required asserts.
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat → the comment’s contract can be violated, i.e. the function may claim a
; trivial identity even when the required pair of asserts is not both present.
